prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some groups; binge drinking patterns persist

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 alcohol use disorder criteria with impaired control and functional impairment; evaluate withdrawal risk

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; AUDIT use variable in APS
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; relapse common without sustained support

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening variable; specialized treatment via CAPS AD and inpatient detox where available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Brief interventions, psychosocial treatment, relapse prevention; pharmacotherapy (e.g., naltrexone/acamprosate) where available; manage withdrawal safely
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Service fragmentation, stigma, and limited long-term rehabilitation and housing support
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS AD services and growing harm reduction/brief intervention programs in some municipalities
